title: How to build a successful aesthetic brand through social media. 4 simple steps to follow.
filename: How to build a successful aesthetic brand through social media. 4 simple steps to follow..en.vtt
excerpt: Relate you into a personal secret I actually hate being on camera hi I'm a massive introvert and I've had to overcome all of these in order to build a multi-million pound business so in this week's show I thought why not share some of the tips and tricks that I've learned to go f…

segments.text: relate you into a personal secret I actually hate being on camera hi I'm a massive introvert and I've had to overcome all of these in order to build a multi-million pound business so in this week's show I thought why not share some of the tips and tricks that I've learned to go from really hating every time the camera coming out to actually feeling quite at home and thus we discuss on this week's show with a very special expert who helped me all the way through let me start by showing you this is my very first marketing video in 2013 and take a look at the glum look on my face it's a very important reason why I look so glum which is that was about six maybe seven hours after I'd started filming and this video this three minute video and it took me all day to get to the point where I could actually sign that video off and I was absolutely Fed Up unhappy and wishing I'd never even started my business at that moment so if you're

trying to build a clinic and a brand a personal brand in particular where people know you as an expert they're actually looking back four steps that you need to implement and on this show I'm going to break down those four steps with an expert on those four steps who is my lovely wife Miranda who has been through all of this with me over the last 15 years you may remember her from some of the earlier shows or she's back here today to share some of the insights about how she's done that through my system but also then setting up her own system and her own social media channels to replicate the same thing and helping hundreds even thousands of injectors to do the same through building their own social media channels I'd like to start with what you think the most important first step is it's doing a video imperfectly because we all imagine in our own heads in our egos that when we start we're going to be like a news anchor and you know be incredibly professional and it cannot be no matter how much talent you have you are always going to be rubbish at the beginning I remember our first

Facebook live we did together and I wasn't appalling but I was sort of like um hello and now we're going to do this and now I'm like boom simply because I had the balls to start and to be brave enough at the beginning to be okay with being rubbish so I remember that live very well I was appalling and I noticed that I looked a lot like that dog who's been offered the cupcakes and is trying really hard to refuse it's so stressed out looking at these cupcakes that it's kind of it looks a bit like a dog on Dad's apartment I got the I gave myself the nickname of Diazepam dog but you mentioned something really important which is The Bravery to start now I've got to thank you for a lot of the stages where you basically orchestrate is something I couldn't get out of so that I had to do it and although I was actually hating it I did it and that desensitizes you [Music] but there's no getting away particularly if you're more on the introvert end of the spectrum there's no getting away

from the discomfort and I think that's what a lot of people stop because of because they feel like the discomfort is a sign they shouldn't be there and actually it's a sign that you're in the right place we believed in ourselves more afterwards even though the delivery was a bit rubbish so that's the first step commit to doing something that usually feels scary but do it anyway and you'll and essentially what you're doing is demonstrating to yourself that you can step out of your comfort zone so that's step one what's num what's number two you have to commit to showing up regularly and the best way that I know of doing this and what many of my students have done is to pick a day something you do in your day anyway you know we all do something with regular whether it be walking the dog or you know doing your morning routine and while you're doing that what I do is I have a piece of paper in our boot room which has all of my dream customers blocks on this so things that are blocking them from taking action and before I go on the dog walk I literally

look at the Block and then I'll talk about it on my Instagram stories then I take that Instagram story put it onto Tick Tock but because I've committed to that small action for my life which is a morning walk I piggyback the content onto it so this is something we debated over the many years and I have now capitulated and I think you are correct and I'm wrong which was I used to have this fixation over quality like I only want to release it when it's really good and you were like you know you just need to make lots of content both are true there are people out there who post once a month and they have millions of followers but for us normals do we have that level of quality and the answer is no this YouTube show is incredibly quality now but when we first started it in 2020 you know we it was just rubbish so we normals have to I believe commit to the small Brave actions regular so that we can find our voice so that it's better to show up imperfectly and then

iterate which we'll go on to talk about than it is to just try for Perfection and hope will get there because we just won't show up at all the biggest thing for me has been about getting the own internal voice out of my head so actually just to be in the moment and speak it's not something that's easy to do what most new people are doing when you first start is you're con you're constantly thinking oh that sounds lame I shouldn't say that oh I said Irma said ah what is so-and-so going to think about this I need to start again and I did do this for a long period of time produce content that never got released because I was so worried that I would somehow reveal my imperfections and no one would ever want to listen to me again and actually it's the exact opposite you have to go through your imperfections and you have to keep talking and that's what I think finding your voice is but there's no way around it by producing your first perfect piece of content the only way around it is to produce imperfect content for a long enough period of time that you become used to the situation and then your content starts to get better the next stage which I know I've seen you do and you've

helped us through as a team collectively which is about systematic systematization and iteration tell me about how what how that works in reality well to make it real for people watching this here now if you have watched this show since the beginning in 2020 you will see that we were in a random office somewhere so we weren't in this beautiful purpose-built Studio then covert hits and because my husband and wife we did it from the shed and literally you can see that it's a shed every time we did a show we just improved one small little thing then we started doing better thumbnails then we started I don't know like our position changed and all these little things meant that over time it grew into this beautiful thing that we all watch now but crucially all throughout our goal was to help someone else it wasn't to ingratiate ourselves and feel better about the content it was always just make it communicate more

clearly and better and this is one of the key things that you as have also helped me enormously with is with setting a regular content schedule that you commit public to so we used to do our Facebook lives so um consistency iteration improve improving a little bit every week instead of trying to get it all right in one go anything else you'd add to that well just to emphasize the point about improving if you were to go to the gym and you said I want big muscles you literally cannot get big muscles on your first session in the gym you have to train you have to tear those fibers and keep tearing them and that's why it's important to show up imperfectly you know I love this quote from RuPaul he said if they don't pay your bills pay them no mind like and that it you've got to think about the people you're serving not about your fellow gym members okay so let's get a bit more practical around what you can actually do to make those early days easier because I know a lot of people you get up all your energy and you make one video it really takes it out of you and

you put it on and it does mediocre or it doesn't do well at all and then you don't look at it again for six weeks how would you suggest looking at the wealth of experience you bought you've built up both personally with your own channel through our business where you've been or you've created many of the systems and then finally to actually training and teaching other clinicians what do you think would be the key step they could make in the early days to get content flowing I'd say if you are shy or an introvert then you feel it's a bit illegitimate suddenly showing up at the camera and saying here are my tips because you think has anyone asked for these tips so the way to oh get over yourself in that circumstance would be to ask a friend get someone who won't judge you could be your mom or you know your sister or something bring them along and get them to feed you questions because then the video or the piece of content is going to be so much more conversational like this is and natural and you are used to talking to patients

consultation Consulting you're good with patience but you think you're not good on camera well guess what if you get a fake patient ask you something from behind the camera then you the answer will be so much more natural so I'm really excited about something that Miranda has done recently which we've been doing some research around what actually drives bookings it's super powerful to realize both the sequence and the set that really work because what you discovered is super interesting around small tweaks that make enormous differences so tell us a little bit about what you've discovered and how people can find out more about it so my passion is social media and injectors getting new business from social media but a lot of times people are they'll try it and it won't work for them and then they lose heart as we've discussed so I thought right I am going to get some actual science behind this and I surveyed 800 injectors and I said how many newbie bookings are you getting a

week from social media and there was a cohort of them who were getting six newbies consistently a week so then my colleague Edwin went through all of these people and looked at their page audited the page and he found that these people getting more than 60 Bookers a week were doing the same four steps and these steps are not rocket science it's nothing crazy but I have now I basically have a backing for what I knew to be true anyway and I'm going to be delivering it and letting everyone know on my webinar doing a new webinar so everyone can find out what these four steps are okay so six is actually a really powerful number because if you do if you get six clients with a 50 attrition rates you're actually losing half of them I've just done the maths off camera and you will actually fully book A clinician's Time completely five days a week 10 appointments every single day within 18 months so that is how you build a clinic from zero to fully booked

within 18 months so that's a really powerful number to aim for if you think you'd like to generate maybe two and a half thousand appointments a year within 18 months make sure you sign up to the webinar [Music]
